summary: Large HSD symbol-labeling pass across sysdolphin baselib assembly, Melee assembly callsites, and a few C/header references. The PR replaced many anonymous func_/lbl_ names with confirmed HSD names, with the author stating the correct names came from Zoids VS, Pokemon XD, and Killer7. The most visible improvements were CObj/camera names, TExp/TEV/display/video callback names, and various baselib API labels, making future decompilation callsites much more semantically searchable.
what_changed: Renamed many anonymous HSD baselib functions and data labels in assembly from func_*/lbl_* placeholders to HSD_* API names.

Applied CObj names across both baselib and Melee callsites, including HSD_CObjSetNear, HSD_CObjSetFar, HSD_CObjSetFov, HSD_CObjSetInterest, HSD_CObjSetEyePosition, HSD_CObjGetFov, HSD_CObjGetAspect, HSD_CObjGetEyeVector, HSD_CObjGetScissor, HSD_CObjSetMtxDirty, HSD_CObjSetupViewingMtx, HSD_CObjLoadDesc, and related getters.
Renamed display/video callback functions in callsites, including HSD_SetEraseColor, HSD_VISetUserPreRetraceCallback, and HSD_VISetUserPostRetraceCallback.
Renamed TExp-related callsites such as func_80384F28 to HSD_TExpSetReg.
Renamed particle/JObj callback use in eflib from func_80374A80 to HSD_JObjSetSPtclCallback.
Touched many assembly files outside baselib so downstream callsites now reference the labeled HSD APIs rather than raw addresses or unknown function names.
Updated several C source/header files to track renamed symbols or declarations, including cobj.h, rumble.h, video.h, util.h, lbvector.c/h, lbcollision.c, and other small references.
In texp.s, adjusted data representation/alignment around constants later discussed in review; reviewer identified constants as 255.0F and 255.0.
smart_moves: Used external shipped games with related HSD code, specifically Zoids VS, Pokemon XD, and Killer7, as evidence sources for correct symbol names instead of guessing from local usage alone.
Propagated labels broadly to callsites across Melee subsystems, which makes behavior in camera, debug, fighter draw, game mode, stage, and library code easier to understand immediately.
Focused on high-fanout baselib APIs; labeling CObj, TExp, display, and video routines pays off in many unrelated decompilation units.
Kept assembly call instruction targets synchronized with new labels, preserving matching while improving semantic readability.
Included header/source updates where needed so renamed HSD functions could be reused from C decompilation work rather than remaining assembly-only knowledge.
decomp_lessons: Cross-title symbol recovery is valuable for HAL/HSD-era code: if another game has the same library routine, importing its name can quickly improve Melee decomp readability.
When renaming high-fanout library APIs, propagate the names to all known callsites in asm and C so future decompilers see consistent evidence.

CObj camera routines are especially worth naming early because their argument patterns reveal camera state semantics: near/far plane, FOV, interest point, eye position/vector, aspect, scissor, and viewing matrix setup.

Data labels should be converted to typed constants when evidence supports it; review noted that raw .4byte values 0x437F0000 and the following double should be expressed as .float 255.0F and .double 255.0 rather than opaque words/alignment.
For large rename-only PRs, keeping an explicit rename list would make review easier; the reviewer asked for one and the author did not have it.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_ prefix for sysdolphin/baselib public API functions.
Use subsystem/object prefixes in HSD names, such as HSD_CObj*, HSD_TExp*, HSD_VI*, HSD_JObj*, matching known library naming style.
CObj setters/getters follow direct property naming: HSD_CObjSetNear, HSD_CObjSetFar, HSD_CObjGetFov, HSD_CObjGetAspect, HSD_CObjGetScissor.
Vector/position camera names distinguish semantic outputs: HSD_CObjSetEyePosition, HSD_CObjGetEyeVector, HSD_CObjGetLeftVector, HSD_CObjGetInterest.
Callback setters use descriptive callback names, e.g. HSD_VISetUserPreRetraceCallback, HSD_VISetUserPostRetraceCallback, HSD_JObjSetSPtclCallback.
Prefer real HSD names over local func_803xxxxx placeholders once corroborated by external matching titles.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed branch targets in assembly callsites without changing instruction bytes, allowing asm to remain matching while carrying better labels.
Used repeated callsite signatures to validate CObj functions: the same unknown address appeared with float arguments for near/far/FOV and vector pointer arguments for interest/eye data.
Recovered baselib data meaning by recognizing IEEE constants; reviewer identified lbl_804DE7D8 as 255.0F and lbl_804DE7E0 as 255.0.
For GAS-era assembly, reviewer said .balign 8 is acceptable instead of a dummy .4byte NULL, but typed .float/.double constants are preferable when the data is known.
Large symbol imports should ideally be accompanied by a generated old-name-to-new-name mapping to support review and future audits.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Asked whether the author kept a list of the renames because it would save review time.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.text: Replied that no rename list was kept.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: `lbl_804DE7D8` is `255.0F` and `lbl_804DE7E0` is `255.0`; should not need a manual `balign`. If using GAS, `.balign 8` is fine, but it would be better to change them to `.float` and `.double`.

follow_up_queries: Find all HSD_CObj* labels introduced or propagated by PR #736 and check whether corresponding C prototypes exist.
Audit texp.s constants around lbl_804DE7D8 and lbl_804DE7E0 for typed .float/.double representation.
Generate old func_803* to HSD_* rename maps for large symbol-labeling PRs to improve reviewability.
Search other HSD-derived games for remaining sysdolphin baselib unknowns in Melee.
Check whether HSD_TExpSetReg and related TExp/TEV labels can help decompile fighter draw or material setup code.
